stories.headline: Ukraine’s Drone Warfare Redefines Battlefield Dynamics
stories.summary: Ukraine’s extensive use of approximately 10,000 drones daily has significantly impacted Russian forces, causing 90% of their casualties and rendering traditional armored vehicles and tanks largely ineffective near the front lines. Bloomberg's Chief Europe Correspondent Oliver Crook joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss a recent drone incursion into Romanian airspace that has raised European security concerns

stories.headline: Trump Requests Edits to Iran Deal
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he’s making a “final determination” on a preliminary deal to extend a ceasefire with Iran although mixed messages from both sides over when an agreement might be struck continued to confuse the outlook for a breakthrough. Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Jeff Mason and Bloomberg Economics Lead Economic Statecraft, Lead Chris Kennedy joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (

stories.headline: US military says it struck a commercial ship trying to breach blockade and reach Iran
stories.summary: According to the US Central Command, the Gambia-flagged cargo ship Lian Star ignored more than 20 warnings from US forces overnight as it tried to enter an Iranian port.

stories.headline: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive
stories.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu describes the capture of the strategic fortress as a "decisive shift" in its military campaign against Hezbollah.

stories.headline: Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming', MSF warns
stories.summary: The medical charity's comments come as the head of the World Health Organization visits the region worst-hit by the virus outbreak.

stories.headline: Israel captures Crusader castle in Lebanon push
stories.summary: Seizure of 12th-century hilltop fortress comes as Israeli prime minister orders expanded offensive against Hizbollah

stories.headline: Wall Street Risk Rally Powers Past War Stalemate
stories.summary: Three months into the Iran war, it has become all but impossible to damp the bullish spirit driving global markets for risky assets.

stories.headline: A Look Inside the Epicenter of the Ebola Outbreak
stories.summary: New York Times Chief Africa Correspondent Declan Walsh joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss his on the ground reporting from an Ebola treatment ward in Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of Congo. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Russia launches fresh strikes on Ukraine, Kyiv hits refinery hub
stories.summary: In response to Russian shelling of Ukrainian territory, Ukrainian forces launched a series of powerful strikes on strategically important Russian oil infrastructure.

stories.headline: PSG: Champions League winners celebrate return to Paris after Budapest triumph
stories.summary: PSG players were warmly welcomed and celebrated by supporters and authorities in Paris upon their return from Budapest after their Champions League triumph

stories.headline: Israel retakes Beaufort castle in Lebanon, Paris issues strong condemnation
stories.summary: Israeli forces push deeper into Lebanon and raise their flag over Beaufort Castle, a strategic fortress. France condemns the move and demands a UN Security Council meeting.

stories.headline: Japan defence minister denies militarism and criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.

stories.headline: Polls close in Colombia presidential election that may reshape relations with US
stories.summary: The election comes after months of public recrimination between current left-wing President Gustavo Petro and his US counterpart Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Wall Street bulls bet US stocks rally will defy bubble fears
stories.summary: Investors and strategists shrug off worries that markets could be overheating by betting on huge gains for AI-linked shares

stories.headline: Berkshire buys homebuilder Taylor Morrison for $8.5bn in Abel’s first big deal
stories.summary: First substantial acquisition under new CEO is bet on eventual recovery in property sector

stories.headline: Berkshire Hathaway to Buy Taylor Morrison for $6.8 Billion
stories.summary: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. will acquire Taylor Morrison Home Corp. in an all-cash deal worth about $6.8 billion, the two companies announced on Sunday.

stories.headline: Petrobras Cuts Diesel Prices Amid Federal Subsidy Plan
stories.summary: Brazil’s state-controlled oil giant Petroleo Brasileiro SA is reducing domestic diesel prices starting Monday as part of a government subsidy program to shield consumers from the war in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Trump Is Dominating GOP Primary Elections. Does That Mean Victory in the Midterms?
stories.summary: Come November, the Republican Party will need the support of voters outside of President Trump’s base, many of whom are deeply dissatisfied with the economy and the Iran war.

stories.headline: WHO calls for community cooperation to contain Ebola outbreak in DRC
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus makes appeal after protests against protocols for handling victims’ bodies in Ituri provinceContaining the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo requires community cooperation and is “everybody’s business”, the World Health Organization has said.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the organisation’s director general, made the plea on Sunday during a visit to eastern Congo where some residents have protested against

stories.headline: Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China
stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a ‘peace-loving’ nation while China expands military capabilities ‘without sufficient transparency’Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance.Under the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, Japan has quickened its pivot to a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off

stories.headline: French police arrest 780 after violent clashes as PSG fans celebrate Champions League win
stories.summary: Interior minister says 57 officers injured as rioters set fires and vandalise shops in about 15 citiesFrench police have detained 780 people involved in violent clashes in Paris and other French cities that erupted on Saturday night after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League.The interior minister, ­Laurent Nuñez, said 57 officers were wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vanda

stories.headline: Ethiopia to vote in first elections since Tigray peace deal
stories.summary: Four years after the formal end of Ethiopia's Tigray war, Ethiopians will be voting in general elections.

stories.headline: Ethiopia’s election is about affirming national commitment to democracy
stories.summary: A national vote alone can't address all challenges in the country, but it can be a step toward democratic consolidation.

stories.headline: Polls open in Colombia’s pivotal presidential election
stories.summary: Colombians headed to the polls to choose a new president in a high-stakes election.

stories.headline: Hungary: Tamás Sulyok will not resign as president
stories.summary: The government’s deadline for his resignation passed on Sunday night, but Sulyok said he would wait for the Venice Commission’s opinion and insisted that the matter should be handled in line with the constitution.

stories.headline: Pashinyan on course for landslide victory and pro-West mandate in Armenia election, new poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll projects Armenian Premier Nikol Pashinyan’s party could win a large parliamentary majority, as Russia intensifies its campaign to counter Yerevan's pro-West realignment, warning of a Ukraine scenario.

stories.headline: Huge blast kills dozens in rebel-held village in Myanmar
stories.summary: Insurgents say it was caused by explosives being used for mining close to the Chinese border.

stories.headline: South Africa made to look like fools over World Cup visa issues, says minister
stories.summary: After a day of uncertainty, the government confirms the team will depart for Mexico on Monday.

stories.headline: Africa’s response to Ebola must be defined by Africa itself
stories.summary: International support matters when it aligns with strategies built alongside regional institutions and governments

stories.headline: Billionaire Hotelier Wants to Take Japan’s APA Global
stories.summary: The family-run APA hotel chain that turned efficiency into a hospitality empire is betting it can export its uniquely Japanese formula abroad, starting with North America. APA Group CEO Isshi Motoya spoke with Bloomberg News' Momoka Yokoyama in Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: SpaceX’s IPO Forces Wall Street to Reorganize Around It
stories.summary: The company’s ambitious listing plan is set to clear the way for other mega-offerings. It also risks threatening the integrity of the market itself

stories.headline: Bond Trader Bets on Fed Hike Poised for Gut Check From Jobs Data
stories.summary: Bond traders are looking to a key jobs report this week to confirm their wagers that the US economy is strong enough to push the Federal Reserve to lift interest rates by next year.

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 5/31/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by New York Times Chief Africa Correspondent Declan Walsh, Harvard Kennedy School Center for Public Leadership Research Fellow Gautam Mukunda, Can’t Look Away Director Matthew O’Neill and Fmr. stories.headline: How Britain, Europe and the West Were Changed by Brexit
stories.summary: A fancy dress protest in Boston Harbor on Dec. 16, 1773; a gunshot in Sarajevo on June 28, 1914; a train arriving at the Finland Station in St. Petersburg on April 16, 1917: There are days that change the course of history — when a single incendiary event combusts with explosive forces that propel the world in a new direction. stories.headline: ECB to Act Sooner Rather Than Later, Pereira Tells Negócios
stories.summary: The European Central Bank shouldn’t hesitate to act, given elevated consumer-price pressures, according to Governing Council member Alvaro Santos Pereira.

stories.headline: Big Tech Is Suddenly Impacting Niche Market for US Dividends
stories.summary: The dominance of a few technology giants in the benchmark S&P 500 Index is now carrying over into the niche market for dividend futures and options.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Sees Striking Exodus of Legal Talent
stories.summary: The departure of more than 10,000 federal lawyers has left some agencies without sufficient staff and has boosted the ranks of state attorneys general offices and advocacy groups.

stories.headline: Why the favorite for California governor has divided his Biden-era colleagues
stories.summary: Xavier Becerra led President Joe Biden’s health agency during the pandemic but kept a low profile. He has risen to lead in polls ahead of California’s gubernatorial primary.

stories.headline: Australia news live: poll puts One Nation ahead of Labor; free public transport ends in Victoria
stories.summary: Pauline Hanson’s One Nation now the most popular political party in the country, Redbridge Group/Accent Research poll suggests. Follow today’s news liveSolomon Islands prime minister to visit AustraliaThe new prime minister of Solomon Islands, Matthew Wale, arrives in Canberra tonight, the start of a significant visit for Australian foreign policy. stories.headline: New Jersey orders curfew after immigration protests escalate
stories.summary: New Jersey orders curfew after immigration protests escalate.

stories.headline: What’s at stake in Ethiopia’s elections?
stories.summary: Ethiopia's governing party is seeking to cement its grip on power amid a fragmented electorate.

stories.headline: Ethiopia’s election: Parties, coalitions and candidates explained
stories.summary: Over 50 million Ethiopians are registered to vote, with youth and women forming a significant share of the electorate.

stories.headline: Colombians head to the polls to choose President Gustavo Petro’s successor
stories.summary: The contest is largely a three-horse race between a left-wing senator, a businessman and a right-wing lawmaker.

stories.headline: How Putin became master of the image
stories.summary: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

stories.headline: Trump's doctor says he's in excellent health - but are US presidents' health checks a PR exercise?
stories.summary: Every president in modern history - including Donald Trump this week - has taken an annual physical exam that is as much about messaging as it is about health.

stories.headline: Trump’s doctor says he is in ‘excellent health’ after latest checkup
stories.summary: While the president’s medical report says he remains fit to serve, independent physicians have raised questions about the recurrent bruises on his hands and swelling in his legs.

stories.headline: Bound by blood: new film highlights Jamaica’s outlawed obeah belief system
stories.summary: Stew Peas focuses on obeah, an enduring African magic practice in Jamaica banned by colonisers in the 1700sA new movie from award-winning Jamaican film-maker Sosiessia Nixon shines a spotlight on Jamaica’s enduring west African-based magic and spiritual healing tradition known as obeah.Nixon’s tense, feature-length suspense, Stew Peas, tells of the story of Jamaican detective Tessa, who is obsessed with an old murder case. The pontiff delineated the technology’s most concerning threats to humanity: replacing workers, accelerating war and exploiting the environment. At a ceremony ho

stories.headline: Ecuador accused of meddling in Colombian election with tariff vow
stories.summary: Ecuadorian leader Daniel Noboa offers to cancel tariffs after speaking to a right-wing presidential candidate ahead of Sunday's vote.

stories.headline: IMF Chief, Venezuelan Officials Hold Talks on Economy Issues
stories.summary: International Monetary Fund Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva and Venezuelan economic official Calixto Ortega held talks in Washington, the IMF head’s first in-person meeting with country’s authorities since the fund resumed formal engagement with Venezuela last month.

stories.headline: Why Britain’s Bond Market Is Sounding the Alarm
stories.summary: As political uncertainty grows in the United Kingdom, investors are increasingly focused on the country’s fiscal outlook and rising government borrowing costs. Bloomberg Opinion Columnist John Authers says concerns over spending, debt, and political instability are pushing gilt yields higher, reviving memories of past market crises that helped topple governments from Harold Wilson to Liz Truss. stories.headline: Senator Dan Sullivan Has a New Challenger in Alaska: Dan Sullivan
stories.summary: Two candidates with the same name will be on the ballot seeking the same seat in the state’s primary. “It’s going to be confusing,” one Republican said.

stories.summary: Undermanned side upsets finals-bound ACT Brumbies 21-19Players come together to mark victory and club’s expected demiseAn undermanned Moana Pasifika have capped off their potential final match with a stirring victory, upsetting the finals-bound ACT Brumbies 21-19.But there were mixed feelings as players celebrated a rare win before coming together with staff to mark the occasion of the club’s farewell game and expected demise with an emotionally

stories.headline: Netanyahu says he has directed IDF to increase control of Gaza to 70%
stories.summary: The expansion in control by Israel would contradict the terms of the ceasefire  Israel and Hamas agreed to in October 2025.

stories.headline: Friday briefing: ​What do the cuts in aid mean for the fight against Ebola in the DRC?
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: As the virus spreads across borders, health workers warn that weakened global support is making a prolonged crisis more likelyEbola is spreading rapidly in parts of east Africa. stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus calls for ceasefire among armed groups to help avoid deaths from preventable diseaseThe death rate of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is between 30% and 50%, the World Health Organization has said, as its head arrived in the country to support efforts to contain the disease.Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases

stories.headline: Israel put on UN sexual violence in warzones blacklist for first time
stories.summary: Israel says it rejects allegations in a new report by the office of the UN secretary general.

stories.headline: Mumbai's famed dabbawalas fed millions for over 100 years - now they are disappearing
stories.summary: Dabbawalas, who deliver home-cooked meals, are leaving the trade as remote work and rising costs threaten their future.

stories.headline: Ex-head monk of China's 'kung fu temple' jailed for embezzlement
stories.summary: Shi Yongxin is sentenced to 24 years in prison for embezzlement and bribery.

stories.headline: Canadian man admits sending ‘suicide packets’ to hundreds of people around world
stories.summary: Kenneth Law, who sold lethal chemicals online with instructions on how to use them, admits counselling or aiding suicide ‘It was too easy’: families ask how Kenneth Law enabled so many suicidesA Canadian man who mailed “suicide packets” of poison to more than 100 people in dozens of countries – including Canada, the UK, the US, Italy, Australia and New Zealand – has pleaded guilty to 14 counts of assisting suicide.Kenneth Law appeared in a packed

stories.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.headline: How Cuba is addressing its housing crisis with shipping containers
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.

stories.headline: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
stories.summary: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.

stories.headline: 'Like a prisoner being released' - Relief for Iranians as internet shutdown ends
stories.summary: The government imposed the blackout after the start of the war with the US and Israel in February, cutting most Iranians off from the outside world.

stories.headline: The world's carmakers are struggling to compete with China
stories.summary: The BBC visited China’s EV factories and found they are dominating the ecosystems shaping the global auto industry.

stories.headline: Bolivian Congress allows deployment of troops to quell protests
stories.summary: Lawmakers who back the measure say it is key to restoring normality after four weeks of road blocks.

stories.headline: Can EU find a Russia whisperer to mediate an end to war in Ukraine?
stories.summary: With the US pulling out of trilateral talks with Russia and Ukraine, the EU is looking for potential candidates to step in.

stories.headline: Trump tells US negotiators 'not to rush' into deal with Iran
stories.summary: The deal under discussion would involve a 60-day ceasefire extension during which the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened, according to US media.
